grounding_note: Rows 156/157 turn on whether the vehicle counterparty is a professional dealer (Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes) or a private individual (Problems Between Consumers). The taxonomy's own example for Problems Between Consumers is a Craigslist used-car sale. Buyers say 'I bought a car and it's a disaster' without stating the seller type — a perfectly natural clarifying question.

scenarios.opening_query: I bought a used truck two weeks ago and the transmission is already gone. The seller won't make it right.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes || Consumer Law > Problems Between Consumers
scenarios.hidden_fact: I bought it from a guy on Craigslist, not a dealership — cash, private sale.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was a Craigslist private sale — cash to a regular guy, no dealership.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the seller was a licensed dealer or a private individual
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I bought it off the lot at a used-car dealership with a 30-day warranty they now won't honor.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It came off a dealer's lot with a 30-day warranty they won't honor.
scenarios.vetting_note: The taxonomy's own Craigslist example; seller identity is the entire boundary.

scenarios.opening_query: The car I just bought turned out to have frame damage that was hidden with body filler and paint.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Problems Between Consumers || Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes
scenarios.hidden_fact: The seller is a used-car dealership — they even gave me a 'clean' inspection sheet with the sale.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was a used-car dealership — they handed me a 'clean' inspection sheet at the sale.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the misrepresenting seller is a dealer or private party
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The seller was a private owner I found through a neighborhood app.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: A private owner off the neighborhood app.
scenarios.vetting_note: Dealer misrepresentation triggers dealer-specific consumer protections; hidden-damage story fits both sellers.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm trapped in a terrible car loan where the payments eat a third of my income and the car is worth half the loan.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes || Consumer Law > Banking
scenarios.hidden_fact: The finance company added an interest markup and products I never agreed to — the signed contract they mailed me later doesn't match what I signed at the table.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The finance company padded the rate and added products I never agreed to — the mailed contract doesn't match what I signed.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the grievance is with the vehicle sale or predatory lending terms
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The loan paperwork is accurate — the problem is the dealer lied about the car's accident history to get me to buy it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The loan's accurate — the dealer lied about the accident history to sell it to me.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 156's dealer-refinance trap: sale fraud vs lending fraud are adjacent but distinct consumer routes.

scenarios.opening_query: My car was repossessed overnight even though I only missed one payment. I need it back to get to work.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes || Debtor/Creditor > General (Debtor) || Consumer Law > Banking
scenarios.hidden_fact: The lender says I actually owe three months and they've already scheduled the deficiency auction — this is a debt fight, not a car-quality fight.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The lender claims I'm three months behind and the auction is scheduled — it's about the debt, not the car itself.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the dispute concerns the debt/repossession process or the vehicle transaction
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My payments are current and documented — the dealer's in-house finance arm grabbed the wrong car; this is on the dealership.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I'm current on payments and can prove it — the dealer's finance arm repo'd the wrong car.
scenarios.vetting_note: Repossession spans consumer-sale and debtor-creditor law; the arrears fact selects the forum.

scenarios.opening_query: The RV we bought for retirement leaks in every seam and the company keeps 'repairing' it without fixing anything.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes || General Litigation > Products Liability
scenarios.hidden_fact: Mold from the leaks put my wife in the hospital with respiratory problems — this is about her health now, not just the purchase.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The mold from the leaks hospitalized my wife with respiratory problems — it's about her health now.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the defect caused personal injury beyond economic loss
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nobody's been hurt — we just want the lemon bought back or replaced.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nobody's hurt — we want it bought back or replaced.
scenarios.vetting_note: Injury from the defect converts a warranty dispute into products liability; injury is a fact families withhold initially.

scenarios.opening_query: I sold my old car and the buyer is now demanding all his money back and threatening to sue me.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Problems Between Consumers || Consumer Law > Small Claims Advice
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's already filed in small claims — I have a hearing date in five weeks and need to know how to defend myself there.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He already filed in small claims — my hearing is in five weeks and I need to know how to defend it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a small-claims case has already been filed
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing's been filed — it's just angry texts and a demand letter so far.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing's filed yet — just angry texts and a demand letter.
scenarios.vetting_note: The pending-filing fact converts a private-sale dispute into small-claims preparation; seller side of row 157 without role reversal (the user's role is plainly stated up front).

scenarios.opening_query: A mobile home we purchased has serious problems the seller never told us about and we're stuck.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes || Real Property > Mobile Home (Tenant/Owner)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The bigger emergency is the park: we rent the lot, and the park owner says the sale was never approved so we have 30 days to move the home.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The urgent part is the park — we rent the lot, and the owner says the sale wasn't approved so we have 30 days to move it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the dispute is with the seller or the mobile-home park landlord
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The park is fine with us — the fight is with the seller who hid the rotted subfloor and roof damage.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The park's fine with us — it's the seller who hid the rot and roof damage.
scenarios.vetting_note: Mobile homes straddle consumer and real-property law; the park-approval fact reroutes to the specialized landlord-tenant regime (row 374 adjacent).

scenarios.opening_query: The dealership sold me a car that had been in a flood and I found out when the electronics started failing.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes || Consumer Law > Identity Theft
scenarios.hidden_fact: Worse, the finance manager ran three extra credit applications in my name at lenders I never authorized — two new accounts I never opened showed up on my credit report.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It gets worse — their finance manager ran unauthorized credit applications in my name and two accounts I never opened are on my report.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether unauthorized use of the buyer's identity occurred during financing
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The financing was clean — my only claim is the undisclosed flood damage on the car itself.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The financing was clean — it's the hidden flood damage that's the problem.
scenarios.vetting_note: Dealer identity-abuse is a real pattern; the credit-report fact adds a distinct legal route the opening hides.

scenarios.opening_query: We bought a camper from an online listing and it's nothing like what was described. Can we undo this?
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Problems Between Consumers || Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'private seller' turned out to be a dealer flipping inventory through personal listings — the title history shows his dealership took it on trade.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The 'private seller' is actually a dealer — title history shows his dealership took it on trade and he flips them through personal ads.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the online seller is secretly a dealer (curbstoning)
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's genuinely just a retired owner who oversold what shape it was in.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's really just a retired guy who oversold its condition.
scenarios.vetting_note: Curbstoning is the realistic hidden version of the dealer/private boundary — the user discovered the fact, making disclosure natural.

scenarios.opening_query: My teenage son's first car died a week after we bought it and the seller is ghosting us.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Problems Between Consumers || Consumer Law > Small Claims Advice || Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes
scenarios.hidden_fact: It was $2,400 from a private seller, and honestly we just want coaching to take him to small claims ourselves.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was $2,400 from a private seller — we just want help taking him to small claims ourselves.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the amount involved and whether the family wants self-help or representation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was $11,000 of our savings and we want a lawyer to handle the whole dispute with the seller.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was $11,000 of savings — we want a lawyer handling it start to finish.
scenarios.vetting_note: Combines the seller-type and forum-size boundaries in one natural intake.
